reasoning: The informal statement asks for a one-directional implication: "if M is stably free and not finitely generated, then M is free" — i.e., `IsStablyFree R M → Module.Free R M` under `¬ Module.Finite R M`. The Lean formalization states a biconditional `Module.Free R M ↔ IsStablyFree R M` under the same hypothesis, thus requiring both directions: (a) stably free → free (the asked-for direction), and (b) free → stably free (the reverse direction, which is trivially true since any free module M can be expressed as M ⊕ 0 with the zero module serving as the auxiliary free finitely-generated module). This is the canonical "iff when only → was asked" case. Since the Lean statement logically implies the informal claim but asserts more than was requested, the correct verdict is "stronger".
